interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with three-sided marketplace
codingTopics: Graph and routing: courier dispatch, shortest path with traffic
Scheduling and intervals: batching orders, prep-time windows
Heaps and top-K: nearest couriers, priority dispatch
Streaming: real-time order and ETA event processing
Hash maps and caching: menu and inventory lookups
systemDesignTopics: Design a courier dispatch system at city scale with batching and real-time reassignment
Design real-time restaurant inventory and menu management
Design ETA prediction combining traffic and restaurant prep time
Design a two-sided order-tracking system for diners, restaurants, and couriers

coding: Given live courier locations and pending orders, assign couriers to minimize total delivery time, allowing batching of nearby orders. Discuss the greedy heap approach.
Compute ETA for an order as prep-time plus routing time on a road-network graph. Discuss how you update it as conditions change.
Maintain a real-time count of in-stock menu items per restaurant from an event stream with bounded memory.
Given a stream of order-status events, detect orders stuck in a stage longer than a threshold using a sliding window.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you balanced the needs of three different user types such as diner, restaurant, and courier.
Describe a time you owned an on-call incident during peak load like a dinner rush.
Give an example of shipping a fix fast under operational pressure.
Tell me about a time you used data to settle a debate about a marketplace tradeoff.
systemDesign: Design a courier dispatch system at city scale with order batching and real-time reassignment.
Design real-time restaurant inventory and menu management across thousands of restaurants.
Design ETA prediction that combines live traffic with restaurant prep time.
values: Grubhub connects diners with local restaurants. Describe a time you built something that helped a small business succeed.
How do you weigh courier experience against diner speed when they conflict?
Tell me about a time you improved reliability for a real-time, time-critical system.
other: How does Grubhub compare to DoorDash or Uber Eats?

Grubhub Interview Guide (2026): Food Delivery Engineering

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Grubhub
grubhub.com ↗

Grubhub is one of the major US food delivery platforms — owned by Wonder since 2024 (acquired from Just Eat Takeaway). The interview emphasizes real-time dispatch, restaurant integrations, and the unique two-sided marketplace dynamics.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a courier dispatch system at city scale

- Design real-time restaurant inventory and menu management

- Design ETA prediction with traffic and restaurant prep time

- Coding: medium DSA, often with geo or scheduling framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with three-sided marketplace

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $140K–$175K total

- Senior SE: $205K–$270K

- Staff: $295K–$385K

- Principal: $410K–$540K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java or Scala (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand operations research and routing

- Brush up on ML for ETA and ranking

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Grubhub remote-friendly?

Distributed across Chicago (HQ), NYC. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Grubhub compare to DoorDash or Uber Eats?

Grubhub is third by US share. DoorDash is the leader; Uber Eats second. Grubhub pays mid-tier; below DoorDash on equity.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, customer-driven, calmer pace under Wonder ownership.
